summary:
An (MSc) graduate in Innovation and Applied Entrepreneurship from the University of the West of England. A results-oriented entrepreneur with a background in strategic thinking, harnessing valuable partnerships and sales generation who also takes pride in my innovative attitude and creative problem solving techniques. 

Runner up for the UWE Bristol Entrepreneur of the year award whilst establishing and running a university born advertising agency.

experiences.title: Co-Founder & COO
experiences.description:
Next-Gen Media is a leading digital advertising network (DOOH) within the education sector and my role is to care of all of our partners to help deliver the best value for their students.

Our mission? To inform, inspire and educate the next generation of young people by empowering education providers to change the way they communicate with students on a day-to-day basis.

A true win-win.

Please visit: www.next-genmedia.com or email info@next-genmedia.com if you'd like to join the DOOH movement.

experiences.title: Host
experiences.description:
I was proud to be selected as the host for the first TEDx UWE 2016 - "The Innovative Attitude" - Saturday 23rd January 2016. This was a great opportunity for me to develop my public speaking, engagement and communication skills. It was a great day with fantastic people speaking from a range of different industries and backgrounds, with a wonderfully responsive audience to go with it. 
 
Further details of the TEDx UWE project can be found on our website. Opportunities for TEDx UWE will include society formation, live streaming, and UWE related initiatives.

Please visit www.tedxuwe.org for more information.

experiences.title: Food and Beverage Assistant
experiences.description: Working in hospitality has allowed me to engage with a diverse range of situations and experiences. It has substantiated my opinion of having high customer service is key in any sector and must be exercised constantly, this has also helped me develop my exceptional customer management and relationship skills.

experiences.company: University of the West of England
experiences.title: Public Speaker
experiences.description:
On the 18th November 2015, I was offered an opportunity to speak in front of up to 200 business owners and UWE representatives at a prestigious distinguished address series. In the few minutes I had I explained the street food model at UWE I was building, my project as a whole, myself (for past-graduate aspirations) and emphasising that the number of student entrepreneurs working with these business owners will increase. It was a great opportunity to develop my public speaking skills and it provided a perfect environment to network. 

Link - I am three minutes into the video! 
https://soundcloud.com/uwebristol/sets/bristol-distinguished-address

experiences.title: Account Coordinator Intern
experiences.description: Work experience at a well established marketing firm in london, concentrating on customer loyalty. In this time I built my network within the company, from outbound strategies to in house design. I learned about their business operations and internal processes, I also worked for ICLP 2 years previous and contributed in meetings with clients such as american express and google, this helped me gain a deeper understanding of how B2B's operate and client management.

experiences.title: Sales Marketing Executive
experiences.description:
On the 8th July 2015, the Start Up Tour Britain came to Bristol. I was approached by Kosta Mavroulakis, Campaign Manager, who knew about what my business does and wanted to get as many students to the bus as possible. I was also a local sponsor and was great PR for myself and Target Student. Here's what he had to say:

"Target student were a great addition to the national StartUp Britain Bus Tour campaign sponsored by NatWest by helping us to effectively reach the student community around Bristol which contributed to our excellent turnout in Bristol."
- Kosta Mavroulakis, Campaign Manager, StartUp Britain

experiences.title: Student Worker
experiences.description: Work experience for a local plumbing firm, developing skills around client management and expanding my knowledge within different industries. I enjoyed my time here but it made me realise that this wasn't the industry that engaged me most.

education.school: University of the West of England
education.description: Whether you are working on your own business start-up idea, or would like to form part of a team looking for a creative solution to a social or business problem, this unique Masters-level programme covers all the skills and capabilities you need to develop yourself as a successful entrepreneur. Its all about learning by doing. The course equips you with all the theories, ideas and concepts you need to be aware of from an entrepreneurial perspective. But more importantly, it also gives you the opportunity to go out and use those ideas by working on a wide range of your business projects. I was involved in the first cohort to graduate from this course in the UK.
education.activities_and_societies: Activities and Societies: Drum and bass DJ on UWE hub radio

education.description: Enabled me to understand the mechanics of business

My most engaging subjects were business studies, Economics, P.E, sociology and english literature. My time at school not only developed my academic understanding of business but also acted as a platform to carry our my various business ventures.

accomplishment_honors_awards.title: Institute of leadership and management - Level 3 Award in Coaching
accomplishment_honors_awards.description: This award allows me to develop my skills in leadership and project management when managing people within a business.

certifications.name: UWE Entrepreneur of the Year Award Finalist 2015
certifications.authority: University of the West of England
